records.ety: [Gr. <?/, fr. Syr. ōkēl damō the field of blood.]

defs: The potter's field, said to have lain south of Jerusalem, purchased with the bribe which Judas took for betraying his Master, and therefore called the field of blood. Fig.: A field of bloodshed.
quotes: The system of warfare . . . which had already converted immense tracts into one universal aceldama.
